title: S.O.725 and S.O.726 dated 03-04-2012-S (CRUSHED BONES)

NTT2.X 3/61/2007-früte Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 2 of 30 -- 3(iih MINISTRY OF COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY (Department ofCommerce) ORDER New Delhi, the3rdApril, 2012 s.O. for the development of the e.xport trade of ind;a, certain probosals for subjecting Crushed Bones. Ossein and Gelatine to quality control and inspection prior to export, were published as required by sub rule (2) of ru;e of the Export (Quality Control and Inspection) Rules, 1964 in the Gazette of India Part ii, Section 3, Sub-section (ii), dated 9th November 201 under the Order of the Government of India in the MinGtry of Commerce number S.O. 2521 (E), dated November 2011; And, whereas, the objections and suggestions were invited from ali Ekeiy to be affected thereby within a pe:iod of thirty days of the date of publication of the said Order in the Official Gazette; And, whereas, the copies of the said Gazette were made availabte to the pubüc on 9thNovember 2011; And, whereas, no objections and suggestions were received from the pub!ic on the said draft; And, whereas, it is necessary to maintain the highest quaiity standards and stipulate health requirements of importing countries and that would encompass t'he standards prescribed at National / Internationa; level, And, whereas, provisions shoulds therefore, be made for a procedure for monitoring to ensure the above conditions of equivalence with reference to the requirements of the importing countries; And, whereas. heaith requirements should be laid down for tne production, storage and transport of Crushed bones, Ossein and Gelatine, And, wnereas, it is the responsibility primarily of the processors to ensure that Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine should meet the health requirements laid down in this order; And, whereas, the Central Government has formulated the proposai specified below for the said purpose and has forwarded the same to the Expor: Inspection Council as required by sub-rule (2) of the rule 11 of the Export (Quaiity Contro' and Inspection) Rules, 1964. Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 3 of 30 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PARTII—SEC. Now,therefore,in exerciseof the conferredby section6 of the Export (Quality Control and Inspection)Act, 1963 (22 of 1963),the Central Governmentafter consulting the Export InspectionCouncil , being of the opinion that it is necessaryand expedientto do so for the developmentof the exporttrade of India,hereby (1) notifies that Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine shall be subjected to quality contrd, inspection and monitoring prior to export; (2) specifiesthatthetypeof qualitycontrol,inspectionandmonitoringshallbe in accordance with the Export of Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine (Quality Control, Inspectionand Monitoring) Rules, 2011, as tie type of quality control, inspection and monitoring which shall be applied to such Crushed Bones,Ossein& Gelatineprior to *port; (3) recognizes; a. national standards of tie importing countries or the internätionät standards. Or b. contractualspecificationsagreed to between the foreign buyer and the provEed that the same is satisfying the health and other requirements of the importing country, And c. in absence of any specifications as mentioned in clause a & b above, Indian standards, And d. in absence of any specifications as mentioned in clause a, b & c above, the standards laid down by a commitee constitutedby the Export ItBpection Council. (4) prohibits the export, in the course of intemational trade, of such Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine unless the same are accompaniedby a certificateissuedby an authoritydesignatedfor the purposeby the Central Competent Authority to the effect that the processing establishment is approved and monitored by the designated Competent Authority and that the consignment of Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine satisfies the conditions relating to its quality control and inspection. [F. Y x 3-ithd (ii) ; (iii) ; (iv) ii) g (viii) Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 18 of 30 -- 3(ii)] 19 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 3rd April, 20 12 S.O. 726(E). —Inexerciseof the powersconferredby section17 of the Export (QuahTy&Tr7aGdGspecti on) Act,1963(22of1963), theCentral Government hereby makes the foltowing rules, namely 1. Shcrf, title and commencement.- (1) These rules may be called the Export of CrushedBones,OsseinandGelatine(QualityControl,Inspection andMonitoring) Rules, 2012. (2) They shall comeinto force on the date of their publicationin the Official Gazette. 2. Definitions:- In theserules,unlessthecontextotherwiserequires,- (a)"Act"meansthe Export(QualityControland Inspection)Act, 1963(22 of 1963), (b)"BoneMill"meansa premise wherebonesarecrushed. A bonemillmayhave integrated collection centre, (c) "Central CompetentAuthority" means the Export inspection Council established under the section 3 of the Act, (d)"CollectionCented'meanspremisesusedfor collectionof bones,hides,skin tendons and sinews of anirnals and their transportation to the establishments; (e)"CompetentAuthority"meansany authorityauthorizedor designatedo, recognizedby the CentralCompetentAuthorityfor approva!andmonitoringof establishment processing Crushed Bones, Ossein and Ge.'atine intended for exports; (f) "CrushedBoneS'meanscrushedbonesof anilTidlsot bovineorigin in different sizes for use in the production of Ossein and Gciatjne and includes degreased bones chips and gel bones; (g)"Director"meansthe Directorof Inspectionand QualityControlappointedby the Central Government under section 4 of the Act; Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 19 of 30 -- 20 3. 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART (h) "Establishment" means any premises where Crushed Bones or Ossein or Gelatine are prepared, processed, packaged or stored, (i) "Gelatine" means the natural, soluble protein, gelling or non-gelling intended for human consumption or not intended for human consumption. obtained by the partial hydrolysis of collagen produced from bones, hides and skins, tendons and sinews of animals. (j) 'Health Certificate" means a certificate issued as per the requirements of the importing country; (k) "Ossein" means the organic matrix of the bone left behind when the mineral salts are dissolved in dilute acid. It is chemically similar to collagen after de rnineralizing the bones with dilute hydrochloric acid, O) "Slaughter house" means the building, premises or place which is approved by the local authority of the State Government or competent authority for the slaugh teing the animals; Basis of cornpliance:- (1) It shall be the primary responsibility of the collection centre to ensure that bones, hides, skin, tendons and sinews of animals are handled, stored and transported in hygienic conditions. (2) It shall be the primary responsibility of the establishment to ensure that the Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine intended for export are handled and processed at all stages of production, storage and transportation under hygienic conditions so that the health requirements laid down under these rules are met and the product conforms to the specifications given in the Order by the Central Government under section 6 of the Act. For this purpose, the establishment shall adopt Good Manufacturing Practices, Good Hygiene Practices and Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point system, as applicable to it. (3) The Central Competent Authority Shail issue necessary instructions from time to lime for registration, approval, monitoring and withdrawai of approval of collecfion centres, bone mills and establishments. (4, Ossein and Gelatine shall not contain residues of notified substances in excess of the permitted levels or as fixed by the importing countries. Conditions to be complied for export of Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine.- (1) Any statutory restriction including commercial, environmental or conservation fneasure imposed ff0h'. time to time by Central or State Government authorities. (2) Raw Diaterials used for the manufacture of Crushed Bones, Ossein or Gelatine must be derived from animais slaughtered in a slaughter house and whose carcasses have been found fit for human consumption following ante and post — Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 20 of 30 -- 3(ii)] mortem inspection or, in the case of hides and skins, from animals found fit for human consumption. (3) The collection centers shall comply with the requirements given in Annexure — (4) The slaughter houses integrated with establishment shall comply with the requirement.s given in Annexure —II. Other slaughter houses must be approved by the State Governments and complying with the norms prescribed by the Food Safety and Standards Authority of india. (5) Crushed bones, Ossein or Gelatine intended for export shall be processed and packed only by an approved establishment. Such establishments shall adopt Good Manufacturing Practices, Good Hygiene Practices and Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point system, as applicable to them. (6) The establishment proc.essing Crushed Bones shall comply with the requirements given in An nexure —Ill. Such establishments shall procure bones only from bone collection centers registered with Chemical and Allied Export Protnotion Council of India (CAPEXIL) or any other authority designated for the purpose by the Central Competent Authority. (7) The establishtnent processing Ossein shall comply with the requirements given in Annexure IV. Such establishments shall procure crushed bones only from bone mills registered with Chemical and Allied Export Promotion Council of India (CAPEXIL) or any other authority designated for the purpose by the Central Competent Authority. (8) The establishment processing Gelatine shall comply with the requirements given in Annexure — V. Such establishments shall procure crushed bones, hides, skin, tendons and sinews of animals only from bone mills / collection centres registered with Chemical and Allied Export Promotion Council of India (CAPEXiL) or any other authority designated for the purpose by the Central Competent Authority. (9) The establishments processing Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine shall comply with the specific requirements of the importing country as per the Instructions issued by the Central Competent Authority. (10) An establishment shall have a responsible person empowered to take ali necessary measures at all stages of production to ensure that all requirements prescribed for the establishment are complied with. 21 Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 21 of 30 -- 2 5. THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PARTII—SEC. 3(ii)] Approval, inspection and monitoring of coliection centres and establishments.- (1) Having satisfied itself that. the estabiishment meets the requirements with regard to nature t.•f activities it out, the C.otnpetent Authority shall accord approval to such establishment. The approved establishment shall be allotted an approval number. (2) If the establishmep;i to carry out activities other than those for which it has been approved, it Shail obtain specific approval of the Centrat Competent Authority prior to the commencement of the new activity. (3) The Competent Authority shall periodicaßy draw a fist of establishments approved by it and forearci the same to the CentrÄ Competent Authority. Subsequent changes inade in the, list shali also be comrnunicated to the Centrat Corr,pelent Authority. (4) The registered bone mils / collection centres and approved establishments shali be cegulariy monitored bv [he Competent Authority. (5) The Competent Authority shall take necessary measures if the requirements cease to be met. (6) The Central Competent Authority shall ensure that the Competent Authority carries out its responsibilities as per the instructions issued by it from time to time. For this purpose, the Central Competent Authority Shail conduct corporate audit of the Competent Authority at least once in a year. (7) The Central Competent Authority shaii notify appropriate authorities of the list of approved establishments. (8) The Cential Competent Authority shall conduct supervlsory audits of the registered bone mills / collection centres and approved establ!ishments to ensure that their registration, approVa\ and monitoring is being carried out by the Competent Authority in accordance with the instructions issued by it and the provisions of these rules. (9) T ne Cerü.ra! Competent Authority and the Competent Authority shall, at all times, have free access to all parts of the coliection centres and approved establishments including their records pertaining to implementation of requirements of this notification 6. Certification .- On request frorn the exporter, the Centrat Competent Authority shall issue health / veterinary certificate, in the prescribed perforrna after satisfying itself that ibe Crushed Bone, Ossein and Gelatine was processed in the approved establishment having valid approval number issued by the Competent Authority as per the requirements of these rules. 7. Fee.- (1) Fee for Assessment: An assessment fee at the rate of rupees three thousand shall be paid by the bone mills / collection centres and rupees five thousand by Osseén and Gelatine establishments to the compefan,t authority every two years. (2) Fee fer Health or Veterinary Certificate. - A. fee at the rate given here under shah be paid by the exporter to the ( •onapetent Authority fcr every consignment of Crushed Bone O c.ad Gelatine intended for human consumption. Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 22 of 30 -- [ qrq 3(ii)] (i) Crushed Bones: Rupees one thousand (ii) Ossein and Gelatine : Rupees five thousand A fee at the rate given hereunder shall be paid by the exporter to the Central Competent Authority för every consignment of Crushed Bones, Ossein and Gelatine intended for technical end use. (i) Crushed Bones: Rupees five hundred (ii) Ossein and Gelatine: Rupees two thousand five hundred However no fee shall be payable for export of bonafide samples not exceeding five kilograms net weight. 8. Appeal: (1) Appeal against the decision of the Competent Authority. (a) Any person aggrieve4 by the decision of the Competent Authority either not to accord it approval under sub-rule (1) of rule 5 or to withdraw approval under sub-rule (5) of rule 5 may prefer an appeal within forty-five days of the receipt of such communication to the Central Competent Authority. (b) The appeal shall be disposed of within thirty days of its receipt, (2) Appeal against the decision of the Central Competent Authority. (a) Any person aggrieved by the decision of the Central Competent Authority either not to accord it approval under sub-rule (1) of rule 5 or not to issue health or veterinary certificate under rule 6 may prefer an appeal within forty five days of the receipt of such communication to an Appellate Authority appointed by the Central Government. (b) At least two-thirds of the total membership of the Appellate Authority shall consist of non- officials. (c) The quorum for any meeting at the Appellate Authority shall be three. (d) The appeal shall be disposed of within thirty days of its receipt. [F. No. 3/61/2007-Export Inspection] DEPINDER SINGH DHESI,Jt.secy, An nexure —I REQUIREMENT FOR COLLECTION CENTERS a) They must have covered place to receive bones, hides, skin, tendons and sinews of animals and their transportation to the establishments. b) They rnust have adequatefacilitiesfor cleaningand disinfectionof the containersin which bones, hides, skin, tendons and sinews of animals are received as well as the vehicles used for their transportation. Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 23 of 30 -- 24 c) d) THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC 3(ii)] They must have storage rooms with hard floors and smooth walls that are easy to clean and disinfect arid. where appropriate, provided with refrigeration facilities. The storage rooms must be kept in a satisfactory state of cleanliness and repair, so that they do not constitute a source of contamination for the raw materials. Annexure — II REQUIREMENT FOR SLAUGHTER HOUSE INTEGRATED WITH ESTABLISHMENT Requirement for Slaughter House 1. The construction, layout and equipment of slaughterhouse shall comply with the followtng: a. b. c d e. f. g h k. Slaughterhouses shall have adequate and hygienic lairage facilities or climate permitting, waiting pens that are easy to clean and disinfect. These facilities shall be equipped for watering lhe animals and, if necessary,feeding them. rhey shall aiso have separate facilities for sick or suspect animals to avoid contamination of other animals. rhe lay out of the lairage sha:i facilitate ante-mortem inspections. The floor shall be water proof; easy to clean and disinfect and laid down in such a way as to facilitate the drainage of the water easily or provided with equipment to remove water. There shall be no water stagnation on the floor. The walls of the meat handling area shall be durable and have srnooth surface which are easy to clean, and impermeable waterproof and tight colored. Walls sha!l be free from projection, all bioes and cables shali be neatly covered. Wail to wall and wall to floor junctions shall be rounded off to facilitate proper cleaning. Ceiiing Shailbe free from cracks, open joints and shall be smooth. free from rafters other than carcasses hanging dev:ces, waterproof, light colored and easy to clean. .A\I doors and windows shall be durable and made of corrosion resistant rnaterial and shall be cf self-c:osing type and easy to c;ean with fiy proofing atrangements. A1!entry points shaftbe providedwith feet washingpit of suitablesize. The pit •nall be providedwith water and disinfectant.The pt•-water shall be changed at frequent interval. Instruments and work.ing equiprnents such as tables, containers, conveyor belts. knives and otner utensils used shall oe of smooth corrosion resistant materials, easy to clean, wash with water and disinfectants. Theymusthavefacilitiesfor d;sinfectingtoolswith hotwatersuppliedat not less than 82 0C, or an alternativesystemhaving an equivalenteffect. The equipmentfor washinghands used by the staff engagedin handling exposedmeat must have •aps desigoedto prevent the spread of contamina tion. Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 24 of 30 -- [qm 3(ii)] 25 2. m. There must be lockablefacilitiesfor the refrigeratedstorage of detained meat and separate lockable facilities for the storage of meat declared unfit for human consumption. To avoid contaminating meat, the slaughterhouses must have a) sufficientnumberof rooms,appropriate to theoperationsbeingcarriedout, b) separateroomfor the emptyingand cleaningof stomachsand intestines; c) instaliations that prevent contact between the meat and floors, walls and fixtures; and d) slaughterlines(whereoperated)thatare designedto anowconstantprogress of the slaughter process and to avoid cross-contanlination between the differentpans of the slaughterline. Where more than one slaughterline is operatedin the samepremises,thereImustbe adequateseparationof the lines to prevent cross-contamination. Slaughter Hygiene 3. 4 5. 6 7 8. 9. Liveanimalsbroughtinto the slaughterhouseshallcomplywiththefollowings a. Only live, healthy animalsshall be brought for siaughtet that met with an accident. b. During their collection and transporation, the annnais shall be handled carefully without causing unnecessary distress. c. Animals showing symptoms of disease or originating in herds known to be contaminatedwith agentsof public heaith importancemay oniy be transponed to the slaughterhouse when the competent authority so permits. After arrivaljn the slaughterhouse, the slaughterof the animalsmustnotbe unduly delayed. Animals must be ciean. Every animal to be slaughtered shall undergo ante-monem inspection by the veterinarian. Stunning,bleeding,skinning,eviscerationand other dressingshall be carriedout without unduedelay and in a mannerthat avoidscontarninatingthe meat. Post-mortem inspection of all slaughtered animals shall be carried in accordance with the instructions issued by the competent authority. After post-mortem inspection: (a) parts unfit for humanconsumptionmust be removedas soon as po'"s.bjefrom the clean sector of the establishment, Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 25 of 30 -- 26 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PARTII—SFE. (b) meat detained or declared unfit for human consumption and inedible by- products must not come into contact with meat declared fit for human consumption; and (c) viscera or parts of viscera remaining in the carcass, except for the kidneys, must be removed entirely, if possible, and as soon as possible, unless otherwise authorized by the competent authority. 10. After inspectionand evisceration,slaughteredanimalsmust be cleanedand chilled to not more than 4 oc as soon as possible, unless the meat is cut while warm. 11. The carcasses must not contain visible fecal contamination and other dressing must be carried out without undue delay and in a manner that avoids contaminating the meat. 12. Carcasses and offal must not come into contact with floors, walls or work stands. Annexure — Ill REQUIREMENT FOR ESTABLISHMENT PROCESSING CRUSHED BONES The establishment shall have: 1 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7. 8. 9 defined cartilage and clean area; no stagnant water or signs of any rodent harborage inside the premises; roads tarred or turfed to prevent windblown dust; adequate covered place to receive raw bones; hard. smooth and washable floor to store the raw bones; proper drainage facility in the raw bone storage area; perrnanent structure costore Crushed Bones; the walls smooth, light colored and without crevices; waterproof and cleanable switches and other installation of the wall; 10.adequatelavatories,changing rooms and wash basins for staff and these shall be away from the main processing portions; 11.adequatearrangementfor protectionagainst pestssuch as insects, rodentsand birds; 12. a drainage facihty to meet hygiene requirements; adequate facility for cleaning and disinfecting the container vehicles carrying Oones, and facilities (or cleaning and disinfecting the containers or receptacles in raw bones are received and the vehicles in which they are transported. Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 26 of 30 -- 3(ii)] REQUIREMENTS FOR ESTABLISHMENTS PROCESSING OSSEIN The establishment shall have: 27 Annexure 1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7. 8. 9. defined cartilage and clean area; no stagnant water or signs of any rodent harbor4e inside the premises; roads tarred or turfed to prevent wind-blown dust; designed in such a way to prevent accumulation of dirt, contact with toxic materials, the sheddingof particles into food and forma_tionof condensationor undesirable moulds on the surfaces; separate covered area to receive Crushed Bones, which is easy to clean and disinfect; adequate lavatories, changing rooms and wash basins for staff, separation between receiving area, processing area and storage of finished produCts; adequate provisions for degreasing and for receiving degreased and dried bone; sufficient productioncapacity for hot water / steam for processing of Crushed Bones; 10.measuring equipment to monitor temperature against time, if necessary, pressure at critical points with adequate safety system to prevent insufficient heating; 11.adequate storage facility for cleaning materials and disinfectants; 12. adequate drainage facility to avoid risk of contamination, 13.waste water disposal system meeting the central / state pollution control board requirements. 14.well-equipped in-house laboratory to conduct analyses or make use of services of externallaboratoriesapprovedby CentralCompetentAuthority; 15. established control for movement of personnel between clean and unclean area; 16.documented pest control program; 17.hygiene control program, which include inspections of environment, equipment and inspection schedules and its results maintained for at least two years; 18. storage area for storing of finished product to preclude contamination, Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 27 of 30 -- 28 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PARTII—sc 19. established and documented cleaning establishment; procedure for all parts of the 20. system to dispose any material suspected or known to be contaminated; 21. provision for separately storing the Ossein intended and not intended for human consumption and are identifiable during production and transportation; Annexure — V REQUIREMENTS FOR ESTABLISHMENTS PROCESSING GELATINE A. Raw Material 1. Forthe productionof Gelatine,anyof thefo!towingrawmaterial/ materialsshall be used. a) b) c) d) e) f) g) ossein; crushed bones; hides and skins of bovine animals, provided they have not undergone any tanning process; pig skins: poultry skin; tendons and sinews; and fish skin, scales and bones. Ossein shali be procured from approved ossein establishments, crushed bones from bone mills and other raw materials from collection centres registered with CAPEXIL or any other authority designated for the purpose by the Central Competent Authority. 3. Raw material procured by the establishment shall be accompanied by a document containing their origin and related inforrnation essential to maintain traceability. B. Hygiene Requirements - The establishments shall — 1 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. 7 8. have a covered space to receive the raw material for processing gelatine; have a clean and unclean sector, adequately separated; be constructed in such a way that it is easy to clean and disinfect; have smooth floor surface and the floor laid down in such a way so as to facilitate the draining of liquids; have adequate lavatories, changing rooms and wash basins with hand washing facilities with running water; have appropriate arrangements for protection against pests, such as insects, rodents and birds; have a waste disposal system; have its own laboratory or make use of the service of an external laboratory approved by the Central Competent Authority to carry out necessary analysis; Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 28 of 30 -- 3(ii)] 9. ensure that persons working in un-cleaned sector do not enter the clean sector without changing their working clothes and footwear; 10. ensure that equipment and utensils are not taken from the un-cleaned sector to clean sector without cleaning and disinfection; 11. ensure that plant hygiene is controlled through regular inspeciion of the environment and equipments; and 12.ensure that installations and equipments are kept in good state of repair and measuring equipments are calibrated at regular intervals. C. Production process: The production process of gelatine shali ensure that: (a) all bone material is crushed and degreased with hot water and treated with dilute hydrochloric acid (at minimum concentration of 4% and pH<1.5) over a period of at least 2 days. This treatment shall be followed either by 29 (ii) (iii) (iv) an alkalir.e treatment of saturated lime solution (pH>12.5) for a period of at !east 20 days with a heat treatment step of 138 oc minimurn during at least four seconds; or an acid treatment ten hours minimum with a heat treatment step of 138-140 oc minimum at least four seconds, or a heat-and-pressure process for at least 20 minutes with saturated steam of 1330C at more than 3 bars ,or any approved equivalent process. (b) other raw materials are subjected to a treatment with acid or alkali, followed by one or more rinses. The pH shall be adjusted subsequently. Gelatine shall be extracted by heating one or more times in succession, followed by purification by means of filtration and heat treatment. (c) gelatine, whether intended for human consumption or not intended for human consumption, is produced and stored in the same establishment provided that the raw materials and production process comply with requirements applying to gelatine •ntended for human consumption (d) the use of presetvatives other than sulphur dioxide / hydrogen peroxide is prohibited. D. Requirernents for finished products: Each production batch of gelatine shali be subjected to tests to ensure that it meets the following criteria Searchable PDF created by OCR.space (Free Version) -- 29 of 30 -- 30 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PARTIPSEC. 3(ii)l 1. Microbiological criteria: Parameter Salmonella 2. Residues: Element Arsenic (As) Lead (Pb) Cadmium (Cd ) Mercury (Hg) Chromium (Cr ) Copper (Cu) Zinc (Zn) Sulphur dioxide (S02) Hydrogen Peroxide (H2 02) E. Identification: Limit Absent in 25 gms Limit 1 ppm 5 ppm 0.5 ppm 0.15 ppm 10 ppm 30 ppm 50 ppm 50 ppm 10 ppm a. The processorshall ensure that Gelatineintendedfor humanconsumptionis keptseparatelyand is identifiableduringproduction,storageand transportation. During storage and transportation, a label shall be affixed / attached to the container,carton or other packagingmaterial,clearly indicatingwhether it is 'intendedfor human consumption'or 'not intendedfor human consumption'. Packagescontaining Gelatine fit for human consumptionshall also indicatethe date of minimum durability. b. Eachexportpackageshallbe legiblyandindeliblymarkedwiththefollowing: (i) (iii) (v) (vi) (vii) (viii) Nameand addressof establishmentwith approval number; Name and address of exporter, if different from the establishment, Batch or lot number; Year, month of processing or manufacturing; Date of minimum durability; Gross weight and net weight; "GELATINE INTENDED FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION" or "GELATINE SUITABLE FOR ANIMAL CONSUMPTION/ TECHNICAL PURPOSES", depending upon the end use; and Product of India.
